title: A2A additions to .fuze/manifest.json
description:
NORMATIVE schema for the manifest fields the A2A card projection and the authZ allowlist read. This is an ADDITIVE extension to the existing per-repo .fuze/manifest.json; it does not redefine the manifest. Fields already present family-wide (repo, tier, expert) are declared here only because the projection depends on them.

IMPORTANT — VERIFIED GAP: `dependsOn`/`providesTo` are NOT universally present. As of freezing they exist on 4 of the surveyed repos (FuzeBI, FuzeExecutive, FuzeSales, FuzeService) and are ABSENT from FuzeAgent, FuzeInfra, FuzeFront, FuzeKeys, FuzePlan, FuzeContact, FuzeHub, FuzeSocial, FuzeDeploy, FuzeSDLC. Backfilling them is a precondition for enabling A2A on those repos, tracked as a devops/platform-governance task — NOT part of this contract PR. Until a repo declares them, the callee MUST deny (see authz.md §4, fail-closed).

repo.description: owner/name. The repo NAME (segment after '/') is the canonical agent identity used as the A2A `tenant` and as the allowlist principal.

tier.description: Selects the projection variant. `product`/`infra` project product cards; `exec` projects an exec-tier card. Repos that serve exec roles WITHOUT being tier `exec` are handled by role-level `metadata.tier == "executive"` (see card-projection.md §5).

expert.description: The repo's `<repo>-expert` agent. Projected into the card description as the authority the callee consults; never exposed as a callable skill.

dependsOn.description: Repos THIS repo may call. Read by the CALLER only as a sanity check; it is NOT an authorization grant — grants come exclusively from the callee's `providesTo`.

providesTo.description: AUTHORITATIVE ALLOWLIST. The set of caller identities this repo's A2A agent will accept tasks from. The callee enforces this; see authz.md. An empty array means 'accept no agent callers' and is a valid, meaningful value. An ABSENT array is NOT the same as empty — absent means unconfigured and MUST fail closed.

a2a.description: Optional per-repo A2A overrides. Everything here has a derived default; a repo that sets nothing still gets a valid card.

enabled.description: Whether this repo publishes an A2A agent surface at all. Mirrors the Helm `enabled` gate (values-interface.schema.json).

servingRoles.description: Role keys (from agent-templates/roles/) projected into card `skills`. Default: every role in the repo EXCEPT `_base` and any role whose manifest sets `coordinator: true`. Order is preserved and becomes skill order.

entryRole.description: The role that receives an incoming SendMessage when the caller names no skill. Default: the repo's coordinator role if one exists, else the first entry of servingRoles.

external.description: If true the card is ALSO published through the Cloudflare tunnel with an https:// interface URL and Cloudflare Access in front. Default false = in-cluster service DNS only.

repoName.description: Bare repo name (e.g. 'FuzePlan'), matching the segment after '/' in `repo`.
